name: DigitalOcean API
what_it_does: DigitalOcean's REST API for managing Droplets (VMs), Kubernetes clusters (DOKS), managed databases, App Platform deployments, Spaces (object storage), load balancers, firewalls, and DNS. Developer-focused with clear documentation and predictable pricing.
use_cases: Provisioning and managing Droplets (VMs) programmatically from agents
Scaling DOKS Kubernetes clusters and node pools via API
Managing App Platform deployments and environment variables
Automating database cluster provisioning and connection pool configuration
Managing DNS records, load balancers, and firewall rules via automation
not_for: Teams requiring deep AWS/GCP/Azure ecosystem service integrations
Very large-scale enterprise deployments requiring premium SLAs and global presence
Specialized AI/ML workloads requiring high-end GPU instances (limited availability)
best_when: An agent needs to manage straightforward cloud infrastructure (VMs, Kubernetes, databases) with a clean, well-documented REST API and predictable pricing — especially for startups or cost-conscious projects.
avoid_when: You need the breadth of managed services (ML, data pipelines, advanced networking) that AWS/GCP/Azure offer, or require enterprise compliance certifications like FedRAMP.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens (full or read-only) are simple and suitable for agents. OAuth2 for third-party integrations. Read-only tokens useful for monitoring agents. No fine-grained resource-level scopes — tokens are either full-access or read-only.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $200 in free credits for new accounts (60-day expiry)
pricing.paid_tiers: Droplets from $4/month (512MB RAM); DOKS from $12/month/node; Managed DB from $15/month; App Platform from $5/month
pricing.notes: Very transparent and competitive pricing with no hidden fees. Simpler pricing than AWS/GCP. API access itself has no cost. Credit card required even for free credit usage.

performance.rate_limits: 250 requests per 5-minute window by default. Can be increased by contacting support.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured error responses with unique id and descriptive message fields. Error IDs map to documentation. OpenAPI spec is well-maintained and machine-readable. Very agent-friendly error format overall.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Droplet creation is not idempotent by default — same-name Droplets do not conflict. Some resources use names as unique keys (domains, SSH keys). Kubernetes node pool operations use IDs for idempotent updates. Agents should check for existing resources before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit of 250 requests/5 minutes can be hit quickly by agents provisioning many resources — implement exponential backoff
Droplet and database creation are asynchronous — agents must poll action status for completion before depending on the resource
No native idempotency keys — agents should check for existing resources before creating to avoid duplicates
SSH keys must be pre-added to the account before specifying in Droplet creation requests
Snapshot operations can take many minutes — agents need long polling timeouts (10+ minutes for large Droplets)
Webhooks are not supported — agents must use polling for event-driven workflows

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on all API endpoints. Personal access tokens support read-only mode for monitoring agents. No resource-level scopes — coarse access control only. DigitalOcean Spaces uses S3-compatible auth with separate HMAC keys. API tokens should be rotated regularly and stored in secrets management.
